Listed six available workflows (bug report triage, feature request triage, decisions kanban, ops board, inbox monitor, knowledge capture) plus an unknown-task fallback.

ok, here's a note to modify your own base prompt in ~/Work/touch-grass repo via a PR 1. since you always need to work with a git repo for your stuff, you should move your workflows into the repo the moment you can and link ~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows so it points to the one in your own repo -- that way when you run in different computers you'll share the same workflow I *think* you have a script to interact with the workflows? perhaps we could simply make the script detect when ~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows is NOT a symlink and give you a fat warning saying "yo, maybe move your workflows to the repo?" thoughts?

Agreed with both points and opened PR #2 on touch-grass: added guidance to agent.yaml to move workflows into a tracking repo and symlink the home directory, and added a symlink-check warning to scripts/workflows.py.
